sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Wolverhampton are in dire straits, sitting at the bottom of the Premier League with no wins in their last 15 matches, including losses and draws in their last 5 games. West Ham United, while also struggling, have managed a slightly better record with 2 draws and 3 losses in their last 5, showing a bit more resilience but still without a win in their last 8.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Wolverhampton have had the upper hand, winning both matches (3-2 and 1-0) against West Ham. These results suggest Wolves have found a way to exploit West Ham's defense, scoring in critical moments, particularly late in games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Wolverhampton's stats reflect their position, with the lowest goal tally (11) and highest goals conceded (40) in the league, alongside zero clean sheets. West Ham, despite their 18th position, have scored nearly twice as many goals (21) but also struggle defensively with 38 goals conceded and no clean sheets in 16 matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Wolves' standout Jørgen Strand Larsen has been pivotal in head-to-heads, averaging a 7.6 rating with 3 goals in the last 5 matchups against West Ham. West Ham miss key midfielder Lucas Paquetá due to injury, while Jarrod Bowen remains a threat with 6 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Despite Wolves’ historical edge in recent head-to-heads, their abysmal current form and league position make West Ham the favorites, aligning with Sofascore's 53% probability of an away win. Expect a tight game with potential for goals, given both teams’ defensive frailties.
